_meta.description: Live macro themes affecting currencies but not on the scheduled news calendar. Update as regime-changing events happen. Scanner reads this at brief generation time and displays it in the daily/weekly review.
_meta.howToUse: Each theme should include: (1) short title, (2) start date, (3) currency implications with brief reasoning, (4) a specific watchLevel that would invalidate the theme, (5) affectedPairs for scanner correlation. Retire themes by moving to _retired[] with an endDate.
_meta.maintenance: Manual updates + Claude WebSearch each session. 2026-07-09: Iran/oil theme escalated — US bombed 80+ Iran targets, Iran retaliated at 85 US sites, 3 vessels attacked near Hormuz, Treasury revoked Iran oil authorization, Brent hit $80.59 intraday.

themes.title: Iran/US escalation — full military exchange, Brent spiked to $80
themes.summary: This is no longer a ceasefire-uncertainty story — it's an active military exchange. US bombed 80+ Iranian targets (air defense, C&C, radar, anti-ship missile capabilities). Iran retaliated at 85 US sites in Bahrain and Kuwait. 3 vessels attacked near Hormuz. Treasury revoked Iran's oil sale authorization. WTI +4.4% to $73.52; Brent hit $80.59 intraday (+8.7%). Brent-WTI spread widening reflects Middle East supply premium. Safe-haven flows now active — USD getting haven bid despite Fed pivot fatigue.

implications.CAD: STRONG tailwind — WTI holding $73+ and Brent >$78 = firm oil support
implications.JPY: STRONG tailwind — safe-haven bid active on escalation
implications.USD: haven-bid returning — DXY may hold premium despite Fed pivot fatigue; risk-off USD-positive
implications.GBP: mixed — safe-haven neutral, but GBP-JPY/CHF crosses see JPY/CHF strength

themes.watchLevel: Brent sustained below $75 = escalation priced out / de-escalation begun. WTI sustained below $70 = CAD leg gone. Brent >$85 = full-crisis pricing, look for USD/JPY spike

themes.title: Fed's hawkish June minutes met with USD weakness — but haven bid may reverse this
themes.summary: FOMC held rates at 3.5-3.75%. June meeting minutes showed a few officials wanted to hike, which should have supported USD but DXY weakened against every G10 except JPY on Jul 8. NOTE: with Iran escalation on Jul 9, USD haven bid is returning — this theme may be muted by the risk-off flow above. Watch CPI Tuesday for the real test.

implications.USD: softening tailwind pre-Iran; now competing with haven bid
implications.CAD: less pure Warsh headwind; Iran theme dominates
implications.GBP: mixed — bounced hard on Jul 8 USD weakness but haven flows now different story
implications.JPY: headwind persisting from carry gap; but haven bid partially offsets

themes.watchLevel: DXY closes below 100 = pivot theme officially over; CPI Tue is the resolver

themes.title: USMCA review + Supreme Court tariff ruling create headline risk on CAD/USD
themes.summary: USMCA agreement review is in progress. Supreme Court expected to rule on Reciprocal Tariff executive orders. Either announcement can hit CAD/MXN/USD sharply and unpredictably.

implications.CAD: elevated vol both directions — soften position sizes
implications.MXN: highest exposure but not in your book

themes.watchLevel: USMCA outcome OR Supreme Court ruling released = theme resolves

_retired.title: US-Iran conflict jump largely retraced — oil hovering near $70-74
_retired.endReason: Reactivated as Trump declared ceasefire over Jul 8; oil back to $73-75 range

_retired.title: Iran ceasefire declared over — oil back to $73-75 range
_retired.endReason: Escalated to active military exchange (US-Iran mutual strikes, Hormuz attacks, Brent $80). Replaced by iran-oil-escalation-2026-07-09.
